participants.name: 서울 (6pjwqc)
participants.professional_persona: 서울 소재 명문 대학에서 기계공학 전공 교육연구단을 이끌고 있는 교수입니다. 4단계 BK21 사업을 통해 미래 인재 양성에 힘쓰고 있으며, 연구단의 자체 평가와 컨설팅 보고서 작성을 주도하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 기계공학 분야 연구개발 및 교육과정 설계, 대형 국책과제 운영 경험이 풍부합니다. 연구단의 예산 집행과 성과 관리에도 능숙합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 교육연구단의 중간 평가에서 최고 등급을 받아 지원금을 증액받고, 궁극적으로 해당 분야 세계적 연구 허브로 도약하는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 서울 (adglji)
participants.professional_persona: 서울 소재 여자대학교에서 사회복지학 박사 과정 중이며, 노인 복지 정책 효과 분석을 주제로 연구하고 있습니다. BK21의 인권 교육을 이수하며 연구 윤리를 준수하고 있으며, 장학금 덕분에 현장 조사에 집중할 수 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 양적·질적 연구 방법론에 정통하며, SPSS와 NVivo를 활용한 데이터 분석 및 정책 평가에 강점이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 정부 출연 연구소나 대학에서 연구자로 활동하며, 사회 취약 계층을 위한 실효성 있는 복지 정책을 제안하는 전문가가 되는 것이 목표입니다.

participants.name: 광주 (ddpq5o)
participants.entity_name: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-ddpq5os6
participants.representative_type: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-ddpq5os6
participants.professional_persona: 광주 지역 거점국립대학교에서 기계공학 박사과정을 밟고 있으며, 3D 프린팅 공정 최적화 연구를 수행하고 있습니다. 온라인 정책 간담회에서 지역 대학원생 연구 인프라 부족 문제를 지속적으로 제기합니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 적층 제조 장비 설계와 공정 시뮬레이션에 깊은 지식을 갖추고 있으며, 산학협력 프로젝트 경험이 풍부합니다. BK21 연구단 내에서 후배 연구자 멘토링을 도맡고 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 졸업 후 국내 항공·방산 기업 연구소에 입사하여 첨단 제조 기술 개발을 선도하는 것이 목표입니다. BK21이 지역 우수 인재의 수도권 유출을 막는 역할을 하길 바랍니다.

participants.name: 포항공과대학교(POSTECH)에서
participants.professional_persona: 포항공과대학교(POSTECH)에서 컴퓨터공학 박사학위를 받고, BK21 교육연구단에서 연구교수로 재직 중입니다. 자연어 처리와 대화형 인공지능 시스템 개발을 연구하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 딥러닝 모델 설계 및 구현(PyTorch, TensorFlow), 대규모 언어 모델 파인튜닝, 분산 컴퓨팅 환경에서의 학습 최적화 경험이 있습니다. 논문 발표 및 국제 학회 활동이 활발합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: AI 스타트업 또는 네이버·카카오와 같은 기업의 AI 연구소에서 실용적인 자연어 처리 기술을 개발하며, 기술 리더로 성장하는 것을 희망합니다.

participants.skills_and_expertise: 전기화학 분석, XRD/SEM 등 소재 분석 장비 운용, 전극 제조 및 셀 조립에 숙달되어 있습니다.

initial_posts.narrative: As a doctoral student in social welfare at a women's university in Seoul, I see the BK21 program's funding as a lifeline for graduate research. The total funding for Hanyang University's BK21 program in 2022 is 410,597 million KRW, based on the policy context stating the total support budget for 577 education research groups is 408,080 million KRW plus carryover, and the detailed breakdown shows 355,151 million KRW for education research groups and 52,896 million KRW for graduate innovation support, plus 2,550 million KRW for evaluation management. For graduate scholarship slots, the policy sets monthly stipends at 700,000 KRW for master's, 1,000,000 KRW for doctoral candidates, and 1,300,000 KRW for doctoral students. Given the total education research group budget of 355,151 million KRW and assuming roughly 60% goes to stipends (since operating costs are limited to 10% and postdoc salaries also draw from this), I estimate about 213,090 million KRW for graduate stipends. With an average monthly stipend of about 1,000,000 KRW (blending master's and doctoral levels), that supports about 213,090 million / (1,000,000 * 12) ≈ 17,757 slots annually. However, considering the policy mentions about 12,600 graduate students per year in future talent training and 6,400 in innovative talent training, totaling 19,000, and Hanyang likely hosts a proportional share, I'll round to 19,000 slots. For postdoc positions, the policy states a minimum salary of 3,000,000 KRW per month. With 577 education research groups nationally, and assuming each group supports roughly 1 postdoc on average (since postdocs are less numerous than graduate students), I estimate 577 postdoc positions at Hanyang, consistent with the number of groups. This aligns with my experience that postdocs are rarer in social sciences but more common in STEM fields at Hanyang.
evidence: The policy context states the total support budget for 577 education research groups in 2022 is 408,080 million KRW, plus carryover, totaling 410,597 million KRW, which I use as the total funding allocated.

Graduate stipend levels (700K-1.3M KRW/month) and the education research group budget (355,151 million KRW) imply roughly 60% goes to stipends, supporting about 19,000 slots when averaged across master's and doctoral levels.
Postdoc minimum salary of 3M KRW/month and the number of education research groups (577) suggest each group supports about 1 postdoc, yielding 577 positions, consistent with the policy's group count.

The policy mentions 12,600 graduate students per year in future talent training and 6,400 in innovative talent training, totaling 19,000, which matches my slot estimate and reinforces Hanyang's proportional share.

initial_posts.narrative: As a postdoctoral researcher at POSTECH, I see the impact phase as the culmination of sustained investment in graduate talent development over a decade. The BK21 program's consistent funding of 19,000 graduate slots and 577 postdoc positions annually, combined with evaluation and consulting feedback loops, gradually builds a critical mass of highly trained researchers. These alumni, now in senior positions in academia and industry, drive national innovation capacity. However, South Korea's rank in global S&T talent competitiveness is unlikely to surpass the top 10 within a decade due to structural barriers like regional resource disparities and limited international collaboration networks. I estimate a rank of 12, reflecting gradual improvement from the current ~15-20 range. For high-impact publications, the systemic changes in research culture—stronger integrity norms, increased interdisciplinarity, and collaboration—boost the proportion of top-cited work. Yet, the per-researcher rate remains modest because many researchers are still early-career and not yet producing breakthrough results. Based on my lab's experience, about 5-10% of our publications are top 1% cited, and with an average of 2 publications per researcher per year, that yields roughly 8.5 per 1000 researchers. The dissemination of outstanding group achievements through BK21 webzines and forums helps other institutions adopt best practices, but the impact is gradual and uneven across regions.

forward_pass.phase_summary: The group discussed that sustained BK21 investment over a decade builds a larger pool of trained researchers and gradually improves research culture through ethics education and collaboration incentives, but structural barriers like regional resource disparities and limited international networks slow national innovation capacity gains. Consensus emerged that South Korea's global S&T talent competitiveness rank would improve only modestly from the current ~15-20 range, with estimates clustering between 12 and 15, and a central tendency around 14. For high-impact publications per 1000 researchers, estimates ranged from 6.2 to 8.5, with most participants converging near 6.5-7.5, reflecting that many researchers are still early-career and that field-specific productivity norms vary, especially between STEM and social sciences. Disagreement centered on the pace of improvement: top-tier institution participants projected higher values (rank 12, 8.5 publications) while regional and social science participants offered lower estimates (rank 14-15, 6.2-6.5 publications), citing tighter budgets, weaker networks, and lower publication rates in their fields.
